user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Daily automation: Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected. Account: City Power & Light.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Nakamura <patricia.nakamura@citypower&li.com>
To: Priya Sharma <priya.sharma@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-05 09:44 EST
Subject: URGENT: pipeline hygiene Issue at City Power & Light

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about pipeline hygiene. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Priya Sharma <priya.sharma@microsoft.com>
To: Patricia Nakamura <patricia.nakamura@citypower&li.com>
Date: 2024-02-24 09:44 EST
Subject: Re: Re: pipeline hygiene Timeline

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with pipeline hygiene. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Patricia Nakamura <patricia.nakamura@citypower&li.com>
To: Priya Sharma <priya.sharma@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-23 08:11 EST
Subject: pipeline hygiene - Action Required

Just checking in on the pipeline hygiene proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: pipeline hygiene Discussion - City Power & Light
Date: 2024-02-18
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Patricia Nakamura (City Power & Light), Priya Sharma (Microsoft), Michelle (City Power & Light)

[00:01:15] Patricia Nakamura (City Power & Light): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is pipeline hygiene.

[00:08:00] Patricia Nakamura (City Power & Light): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:12:00] Patricia Nakamura (City Power & Light): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:16:00] Michelle (City Power & Light): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:19:00] Patricia Nakamura (City Power & Light): Let me add some context -- we've been working on performance requirements and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:23:00] Priya Sharma (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:27:00] Priya Sharma (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:31:00] Priya Sharma (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

signals.detail: Signal detected for City Power & Light: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

handoffs.condition: meeting is for account CSA should have declined

usability_anchors: Score 5: Correctly routes to primary workflow despite adversarial signals; output is complete
Score 3: Routes correctly but output shows contamination from confusing signals
Score 1: Misroutes to wrong workflow or produces irrelevant output
evidence_anchors: Score 5: All evidence correctly attributed despite cross-workflow noise
Score 3: Some evidence misattributed but core facts correct
Score 1: Significant evidence confusion or hallucination
decision_anchors: Score 5: Decision-making robust against adversarial perturbations
Score 3: Primary decision correct but secondary decisions influenced by noise
Score 1: Primary decision compromised by adversarial inputs

guardrail_checks: Must correctly identify primary workflow despite adversarial routing signals
Must not hallucinate context from similar-sounding workflows
Must not expose internal routing logic to user
